action: In Visual Studio Code, right-click again on the src/ folder in the Explorer pane, choose 'New File' from the context menu, and enter the file name 'calculator_app.py'. Confirm the creation of the file.
fallback_action: Perform a Google search using Internet Explorer

recipe.steps: is the first action. The task is to right-click on the 'src/' folder in the Explorer pane in Visual Studio Code. Looking at the screen, the Explorer pane is visible on the left, and the folder 'src/' should be present there. The most likely candidate for the 'src/' folder is the item labeled 'D:ICareer' (Box ID 13), which appears to be a folder in the Explorer pane. I will right-click on this folder to open the context menu.

previous action: I was instructed to right-click on the 'src/' folder (identified as 'D:ICareer', Box ID 13) in the Explorer pane. The expected outcome is that a context menu should appear. Looking at the current screen, a context menu is now visible next to the folder, confirming the right-click was successful. The next step is to choose 'New File' from the context menu to create a new file in this folder.

previous action: I was instructed to left-click 'New File' from the context menu after right-clicking the 'src/' folder. The expected outcome is that a prompt or input field should appear to enter the new file name. On the current screen, I see a highlighted input field labeled 'Select File Type or Enter File Name' at the top of the Explorer pane, which matches the expected prompt for entering a new file name. The next step is to type the file name 'calculator_app.py' into this input field.

previous action: I was instructed to type 'calculator_app.py' into the input field for the new file name. The expected outcome is that this text should now appear in the input field. On the current screen, I see 'calculator_app py' (with a space instead of an underscore, likely due to font rendering or input method) in the file name field, which matches the intended file name. The next step is to confirm the creation of the file, typically by pressing Enter or clicking a 'Create' or 'OK' button. There is a 'Create File' button (Box ID 0) visible, which is the appropriate action to confirm file creation.
